entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori RAP display [object Object]
entry.content:
Hello @junwu,

After successfully deploying SAP Public Cloud, this issue still appears.

Strangely, after refreshing the browser, the custom app works normally.

Is this issue related to the SAP Public Cloud 2502 upgrade?

entry.title: Nominal Separator
entry.content:
Hi All,

Can we add dot separator to value in Compare Supplier Quotation App?

For example 1.000.000, 1.050.000, 1.100.000, etc.

Thank you!!!

#SAPS4HanaCLoudPublicEdition

entry.title: Re: CDS View accessing external system remotely in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello SAPILMGUY,

The data which is accessible via SDA/SDI are the target.

For S/4HANA, it seems HANA and IQ could be the storage of data archiving in SAP ILM Retention Management. But I don't know whether or not or How.

https://help.sap.com/docs/PRODUCT_ID/35d6f7d8cbd04dbf997ca36785c7a795/d74811ee0df94808a328779a2e31dcfc.html

Kind regards,

Masa

entry.title: Re: Guidelines when creating CAP Application?
entry.content:
There are best practises for CAP Application development here :

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/about/best-practices

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/best-practices

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/providing-services#best-practices

and lot of other resources, guides etc.

entry.title: Is there a benchmark performance for openCypher query vs sql join query for data in SAP HANA Graph?
entry.content:
Hi All , 

Basically  we are evaluating what kind of performance gain we can expect on using open cypher query instead of simple sql joins .

We are mainly focusing on retrieval time for filtering in 1Million-10Million rows and around 500-1000 columns. 

Also is there a performance impact on using homogenous graph and heterogenous graph . If yes are there any readings for the same ?

Help is highly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Issue data blending in Story using table
entry.content:
Hi ,

I think you are using optimized story experience.  Please note the "Blending" option for chart is available currently, however 'Blending' for tables - are yet to be released. its in the roadmap. 

Please note the blending options for classic story is available. if your scenario demands that option is .

Please refer unsupported features as of today : 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/2d00f5ff0c9245a99a9df96cb83236a9.html

 

Workaround : 

1. as a work around use the classic story. 

2. perform the joining in the back end and use it the joined model in the front end.

Thanks with regards,

Giri

SAP

entry.title: Re: CDS View accessing external system remotely in S/4HANA
entry.content: @Masaaki - thanks for the quick reply. the traditional approach to read data from archive files .. a sample is here. . https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/data-archive-retrieval-from-archived-file-in-custom-reports-part-1/ba-p/13545912  the storage of the files is agnostic as they can be stored on any compatible ILM store or file system as well without ILM.

entry.title: [MIA] My IDE Actions
entry.content:
Introduction

Since release 2502, the ABAP environment has included IDE Actions. This allows you to extend the ABAP development tools with your own actions. Various standard actions are available for this, such as inputs, side effects and outputs. The implementation is carried out in the backend in ABAP OO and can be tested directly. The SAP Help documentation gives you an easy introduction to the topic of implementation and if you want to see the whole thing live, there is a recording with details from the ABAP Environment Roundtable #20.

 

Challenge

In everyday life we ​​try to follow best practices, we implement an additional interface for our classes, create a factory to decouple our actual implementation and if we want to write clean ABAP unit tests, we need an injector for the factory. Basically, we create the same objects over and over again.

 

Implementation

Let's take a look at the development and the frameworks used. In this article, we will not go into every detail of the development.

 

IDE Action

The framework provides various options for implementing your action. In the first step, we define a trigger for when our action is available.

 

This should become active when exactly one package has been selected or is in focus. In the second step, we create a UI because we need various information from the user. Since we suggest the names for the objects, they should be recreated when certain fields are changed. The side effects are available for this.

 

As a result, we receive a message as an HTTP popup. Here you can find information about the transport and the objects created.

 

XCO Library

Finally, we use various XCO libraries to, for example, access the corresponding transport of a package or to generate the various objects in the system. The XCO libraries contain many useful components for reading and writing ABAP artifacts, but also helper classes for strings, messages or system fields.

 

Process

Some more details about the process and the procedure:

  • Determination - From the selected package we can deduce which namespace we are in (Y, Z or /NSPAC/). If the package is new and assigned to a transport request, we can derive the transport and assign it in the form.
  • Generation - A class can be created using the generator; interface, factory and injector are optional. If you delete the entries, the corresponding object will not be created. But be careful, some objects are dependent on each other.

 

Open Source

We make the current development of MIA available as open source. In our GitHub repository you can find further information on installation and the features related to the actions. If you want to learn how to build IDE Actions, take a look at the official documentation or take a look at the project to see how we solved it.

 

Conclusion

With IDE Actions, SAP finally provides ABAP developers a simple way to adapt their IDE to their own requirements and even with ABAP. You initially have a large framework at your disposal with which you can carry out many everyday tasks while remaining clean core and cloud ready.

entry.title: Uplevel Your Integration Along Your ERP Transformation
entry.content:
Are you planning to move towards SAP S/4HANA Cloud and wonder what to do with your existing integration scenarios along this transformation journey? Integration is the central nervous system of each enterprise which can make or break your supply chain and can deliver a delightful or disrupted experience for your customers. Hence, it is essential to revisit your integration strategy in context of ERP modernization which will allow you to realize the full benefits of SAP S/4HANA Cloud in future. This is the perfect time to elevate and consolidate legacy integration technology, to reduce TCO and benefit from robust, scalable modern future proof integration architecture.

The new white paper Thinking ahead with an integration strategy for a future-proof transition to SAP cloud ERP can help you to define a joint strategy which addresses both the technical and organizational dimension of integration modernization.

 

Let me walk you through the key highlights of this paper…

Modernizing your business through a clean core

Many SAP customers have extended their SAP ERP system to meet specific business requirements. This often led to technical depth which can make future upgrades and migrations complex. To overcome this situation SAP recommends following a clean core approach: An SAP S/4HANA Cloud solution has a clean core when it is up to date, documented, unmodified, consistent, efficient, and has custom extensions decoupled from SAP source code. The clean core approach has multiple dimensions. With regards to the integration dimension the objective is to keep your IT landscape reliable and flexible using standard APIs and events.

You can accomplish this objective with the help of SAP BTP: This platform offers a comprehensive set of integration capabilities which allows you to integrate business processes end-to-end across SAP, partner and third-party solutions using public APIs and events.

Mapping an integration strategy in five steps

For defining a future-proof integration strategy along your ERP transformation you simply need to follow five steps:

Let's take a quick look at each step:

1. Explore modern integration concepts

Follow an API-led integration approach which can increase flexibility and scalability – not only from a technical but also from a business point of view:

  • API services: API management allows you to share and socialize APIs with stakeholders inside and outside of the organization while controlling access and enforcing associated security policies. Accelerated onboarding of new API consumers avoids bottlenecks during parallel rollouts, while the complexity or changes of the underlying process is hidden from the API consumer.
  • Event-driven approach: An event-driven integration approach enables real-time information sharing for process automation and analytics in a loosely coupled fashion. Such an architecture can scale up flexibly in many dimensions such as adding more event providers and consumers or handling higher loads.
  • B2B integration: Replace legacy B2B technologies and services with a simple, scalable implementation for thousands of trading partners. Leverage AI for automating migrations which also allows you to replace legacy interfaces with modern APIs.

2. Define a modern enterprise integration strategy

Next, you define an integration strategy comprising of the following key ingredients:

  • SAP Integration Suite: This integration platform as a service solution enables you to quickly connect systems, automate business processes, and harmonize experiences across the extended enterprise.
  • SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology: Leverage this proven approach to achieve integration excellence and define your integration strategy as a C-level priority.
  • SAP LeanIX solutions: Optimize your enterprise architecture, including interfaces and integration technologies, with management solutions that help you meet changing needs.
  • ERP clean core strategy: Realize this strategy by integrating and extending SAP S/4HANA Cloud to keep the landscape reliable and flexible.

The graphic below summarizes the key requirements for achieving a clean core with regards to the integration dimension:

3. Identify the right timing for modernizing your enterprise integration architecture

When transitioning toward SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can embark on one of the following options about when to modernize your integration approach. The figure below shows some pros and cons for each option. Check out the white paper for further ones, including suitable use cases for integration modernization per option.

When making a decision on when best to modernize your integration architecture you also need to consider where you are coming from: For greenfield implementations of SAP S/4HANA Cloud having a “clean” system from the start on you should focus on modern integration concepts right from the beginning on. In case of a brownfield implementation of SAP S/4HANA Cloud you have a heritage of existing integration scenarios in place: Hence, a phased approach for modernizing integrations seems to be more appropriate. You should consider adopting modern integration concepts for defined use cases before, while and after transitioning toward SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

4. Enable an agile integration practice

Adopting a modern approach on enterprise integration involves not just technology changes, but also significant shifts in how an organization operates. For achieving integration excellence, you also need to address the organizational dimension of enterprise integration. Hence, the SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology also includes best practices for enabling such an agile integration practice, such as introducing an integration center of excellence, integration governance, and integration quality assurance.

5. Club integration and cloud transformation together

For a successful cloud transformation, you need to think, plan and execute your ERP and integration modernization together. To better understand the interdependencies of both, SAP LeanIX solutions for enterprise architecture management can provide you with the needed insights: To get an overview of existing interfaces, you can import your SAP integration scenarios into SAP LeanIX with the help of a predefined integration content package for SAP Integration Suite. Th architecture and roadmap planning feature of SAP LeanIX allows you to visualize the impact on interfaces between applications along a planned transformation such as moving toward SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Furthermore, SAP also provides tools and services to support to migrate toward SAP Integration Suite in an automated fashion.

Enabling innovation through integration

Finally let’s look at the value of a modern integration strategy with examples of business benefits, backed-up with customer examples.

 

  • Digitalization allows you to embrace new business opportunities by digitalizing your business processes within and beyond your organization.
    Look at how F. Hoffmann-La Roche Ltd transforms the patient journey with digital solutions.
  • Business agility helps you to adapt your business quickly to ever-changing market requirements.
    Look at how Mahindra creates a future-ready IT infrastructure in the cloud with the RISE with SAP Solution and SAP BTP.
  • Real-time business process, insights, and actions delivers instant value to your customers while staying ahead in today’s competitive market and remove information silos and fragmented data.
    Look at how CONA Services embraces digital capabilities in the cloud to boost bottlers’ operational efficiency and competitive advantage.
  • Scalability allows you to grow your business as you go by managing peaks and expanding business network confidently.
    Look at how Żabka Polska accelerates retail digitalization with integrated and agile processes.
  • AI empowerment to accelerate integration modernization at higher quality with the help of generative AI.
    Look at how City of Hamburg distributes funding grants faster with an AI-enabled technology platform.

Conclusion

No matter which route you take for getting toward SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Think and plan modernizing your ERP and integration architecture together. Make yourself familiar with the steps to take by reading the white paper Thinking ahead with an integration strategy for a future-proof transition to SAP cloud ERP.

Share with us your experiences with modernizing your integration approach when transitioning toward SAP S/4HANA Cloud by leaving a comment to this blog post: What is the best timing? What are good use cases for applying modern integration concepts? Which business benefits were achieved?

entry.title: SAP Business Accelerator Hub domains take centerstage
entry.content:
Previous articles in this series 

  • What are business accelerators? 
  • SAP API Business Hub renamed to SAP Business Accelerator Hub 
  • Introducing SAP Business Accelerator Hub Domains

A brief recap

SAP Business Accelerator Hub now hosts a variety of content catering to, sometimes diametrically opposite persona like pro-code developer or a no code user. To ensure all users have a great experience on the site we introduced the concept of domains.

We liked it so much we removed all our previous navigations and made it our top level navigation. That is what this blog covers.

Moving domains to centerstage

We started with Integration and SAP Build domains, then we added Analytics and Transformation. 

We like this idea so much and believe in it that we moved this up to the top menu!

So where did the other menu items go? Under the Explore menu item on the top right of the window!

I really love this screen shot below, it shows 90% of the valuable things you are able to do with the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

 But this does not answer where did 'Discover Integrations' go?

It is the landing page of the Integration domain which fits right? If i am interested in integration that is possible the first place i would like to be.

entry.title: Re: New Videos on DMS with SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
I have to attach the invoice with API TRIGGER in the approval form specifically not using Form Trigger. Kindly post anything related to it.
#Sap Btp build process automation

entry.title: Re: How to change a numeric value to a percentage value i...
entry.content:
Hi,

This is very much possible. create a two widgets one with numeric and another one widget using %.

hide the % widget by default.  create a push button,in this push button write a script 'on click'  event.

widget2.visible = true. ( widget1.visible = false).

In this way you can control the widgets. Please note this can be achieved using : Optimized story experience mode only. meaning the latest recommended SAC Story. (which comes with advanced mode of scripting).

thanks with regards

Giri

entry.title: Re: How to assign value to oApplication object
entry.content:
Hi ramco1917,

You need to make sure that your UI API connection is successful before using oApplication. UI API connection can be made as below:

 

SAPbouiCOM.SboGuiApi SboGuiApi = null; 
string sConnectionString = null; 
SboGuiApi = new SAPbouiCOM.SboGuiApi(); 
sConnectionString = System.Convert.ToString( Environment.GetCommandLineArgs().GetValue( 1 ) );
SboGuiApi.Connect( sConnectionString );        
SBO_Application = SboGuiApi.GetApplication( -1 );

 

If you want to display only the MessageBox, you can use some other option. For example:

MessageBox.Show("Connected!");

Hope it helps!

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori RAP display [object Object]
entry.content: Do a cross check on the semantic objects. Redeploy and cross check the CDS artifacts. It should work fine.

Regards,

Dear Krishna,

Thank you for your question. You can refer to the following documentation on SAP Help Portal to configure the output of payment advices through Email channel: 

Configuring the Output of Payment Advice Forms: Email Channel 

 

Best regards,

Sharon

If user is creating Invoice within State i.e SGST then it should be IntraState Tax Code at both places.

entry.title: Re: How to assign value to oApplication object
entry.content:
Hi Ankit

If I make connection as per my code then also it is to be done same way.

Only Command line arguments is the way.

Thanks

entry.title: SAP CPI - Creating a Passthrough Scenario for EDI data (X12/EDIFACT) via TPM
entry.content:
SAP Integration Suite has launched passed through scenarios for EDI files in Q2-2025. This was a much awaited feature in SAP IS/CPI where we can route a message via B2B monitoring ( TPM standard package - V2) without executing the mapping ( Custom/MAG).

Below scenario illustrates the setup when we want to do passthrough of EDI content from your own company to Trading Partner from SAP CPI.

System/Subsidiary --> SAP IS/CPI --> Trading Partner

Step 1: Under your Own Company. Go to existing system or create new system.

Step 2: Assign a type system version that your want to by pass. Here, I am taking EDI810-004010.

Step 2: Create a outbound Communication Channel ( Process Direct Sender) that will feed the message to Step 1B of TPM flows.

 

Step 3: Create Trading Partner and add the Identifier of Trading Partner.

Step 5: Add Receiver channel ( Process Direct/AS2/SOAP) in Trading Partner System, based on your required protocol. Here I am routing to one of the process direct receiver.

Step 6: Create a Pass through template.

 

Step 7: Create transaction Passthrough with Checkbox Passthrough

 

Step 8: You will see Passthrough scenario. Data Originating from your company and going out to Trading Partner. No mapping is needed in this transaction.

Step 9: Create an agreement from above Template.

 

Step 10: Fill all the details of agreement.

 

Step 11: Assign the respective sender and receiver Process adapters in the communication Channel tab. You can request for acknowledgement from your trading partner or receiver system.

 

Step 12: Assign the receiver channel. You need to assign the FA receiver channel too.

Step 13: Don't forget to put Message type in receiver interchange, here I am putting 810

 

Step14: Now you are done with your passthrough. Activate your agreement. 

Now, you are good to test the scenario for a passthrough.

Note: Right now, passthrough scenarios are only available for X12 and EDIFACT.

References:

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/creating-pass-through-b2b-transaction?version=CLOUD

entry.title: Re: New Videos on DMS with SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
@Sonia_00 Can you explain more what you need to do? An approval form can take a file field just like a regular or trigger form. 

Are you saying you have an API trigger and you want, as part of the trigger, to include the invoice? You should put the invoice in DMS and then pass as part of the API trigger the objectID.

entry.title: Re: Live profile view Variable Pay-Enhancement
entry.content:
Hi @rkumar1947 ,

Live Profile View will respect field-based permissions.  I suggest you use this to display the appropriate fields to the employees via the standard 'Everyone' group.  All fields must exist in the template to display them via Live Profile view.

Hope this helps.

Christian

entry.title: Re: Budget Configuration: Prevent Exceeding Budget at Cou...
entry.content:
Hi @MariaCarlotaAbreu ,

To my knowledge, this is not possible.  Budget rules apply to the population on the worksheet and cannot be segmented in such a way using groupings.

Depending on the number of countries, you could try to use custom config to create one budget per country and remove the grouping. The rules can be applied to each budget this way though you will have more  config in the template as a result as each budget requires custom columns.


Thanks,

Christian

entry.title: how to create time-based Condition Monitoring Plan
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

How to create time-based Condition Monitoring Plan or can strategy be used in Measuring point to create Strategy based plan.

entry.title: Re: AI Assisted Skills Architecture Creation
entry.content:
@Marcos_Ramos 

Regarding the question on inferred skills that are in English and not related to the job profiles: 

we would recommend raising a support ticket from the instance. This will help us to look into the logs and profiles to help explain the inferences.

Thank you,

Kanchana

entry.title: Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Apps.
entry.content: I can not subscribe to SAP Build Apps when I add it from service markketplace.

entry.title: Re: How to create a simple SAP GUI using SAP Process Automation.
entry.content: Hello, can you specify what do you mean by GUI? and what specific issue are you experiencing creating your first automation?

entry.title: Re: New Videos on DMS with SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
@Dan_Wroblewski 

I have uploaded the invoice to DMS and passed its Object ID in the automation. However, when I pass this Object ID to the approval form, the document is not displayed.
When I pass below like this,

spa-res:cmis:folderid:**********************************************

the approval form works correctly.

However, when I store the folder ID in a variable and pass it dynamically from the automation to the approval form, it does not work.

I have gone through multiple blogs and videos, and I noticed that everyone is using a form trigger instead of an API. If you could try it out and help me with this, it would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Digital Signature Verification
entry.content:
Hello,

 

For DocX (Document Information Extractor), there is no capability as far as I am aware of. You need to use third party apps/api to do the validation for you.

 

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Is "Amount Split" tab available in the Fiori screen o...
entry.content: Hi, There is no "Amount Split" Tab in the app, could you help to clear where did you see the tab? I can only see there is the manual amount split supported in the app Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced. Please check the following link for further reference, https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/c7c55da4af0a49d08d7df04fc5b39138.html?locale=en-US&version=2502.500 Kind Regards, Lucy

entry.title: Re: Some ways to replace nodes in SAP HANA graphical calculation view without losing metadata
entry.content:
Hello,

there's another (well hidden) option: You can drag new aggregation and drop it to arrow connecting relevant nodes.

After confirmation you get the desired outcome. All mappings and calculations in top nodes stay untouched.

entry.title: How to prepare SAP Certified Professional - Solution Architect - SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi Guys,

Does anyone has this certificate, SAP Certified Professional - Solution Architect - SAP BTP, SAP Certified Professional - Solution Architect - SAP BTP?

Can you share how to prepare for this certificate?

I see there is learning journey, Becoming an SAP BTP Solution Architect for this certificate, and I have doubt if it is enough? 

If I go through that learning journey, can I pass the exam and get the certificate?

In addition, I found there is no any hands on practice in the learning journey, I am afraid it is much far to pass the exam, if only complete this learning journey.

entry.title: Re: Responsible for creating an inbound delivery in SAP S/4
entry.content:
Hi,

Purchaser, Warehousclerk...

Ask who is doing the pysical work and who is doing the paper work.

KR

Robert

entry.title: GRC Tuesdays: Connect the dots at the 2025 SAP GRC conference in Budapest!
entry.content:
Once again we are proud to be working with TAC Insights to bring together our two popular GRC conferences on internal control, compliance and risk management and on cybersecurity and data protection into a one stop shop event for all Governance, Risk, and Compliance & Security professionals. SAP for Internal Controls, Compliance and Risk Management is the not-to-be-missed event for business and IT professionals in the SAP GRC ecosystem: customers, prospects, and partners.

 

What can you expect?

Let’s just start with a few facts:

  • 150+ delegates from across a wide range of industries, representing SAP customers, prospects, partners, and solution experts
  • Highly interactive format with opportunities for 1:1 meetings, round tables, solution demos and more
  • Exclusive, business focussed insights, actionable solutions and lessons learned directly from established SAP customers

 

Insightful keynotes

In the conference opening keynote Connect the dots, Axel Vetter – Head of Quote-to-Cash & Governance, Risk Management, and Compliance Product Marketing at SAP, will share his perspective on what the new risk landscape looks like, and what SAP is doing to help companies bring out their best. This of course includes sharing the highlights of SAP’s roadmap for GRC & Security solutions.

We will then have a keynote by the event’s platinum sponsor delivered by Richard Hunt – Founder and CEO Turnkey Consulting, and Chris Johnston – Head of Office of the CFO Advisory for EMEA SAP, on the topic of People, protection, performance: elevating security as an enabler of business growth. Richard and Chris will challenge common misconceptions about the business value of security and showcase real world examples of what’s possible when people, protection, and performance come together.

 

Real life case studies from users

Some examples of real-life use cases from companies across industries and geographies that will be presented during the course of these 2 days include:

 

Shell

An exploration of the company’s ongoing journey from manual controls to digitally enabled efficient controls through SAP Process Control.

 

Focusing on how Siemens Healthineers protects its critical SAP assets proactively in a global environment on its transformation to SAP RISE with the help of Onapsis.

Siemens Healthineers

 

Postnord

An overview of Postnord’s technology landscape, its SAP implementation process of SAP Identity Access Governance, the resources used, and a detailed insight into “how they did it”.

 

How, by leveraging SAP Audit Management and SAP Process Control, internal audit departments can enhance the efficiency of audit processes, improve the risk-based audits, and simplify the follow-up on actions.

OYAK Mining Metallurgy

 

CSM Ingredients

Getting behind the scenes of CSM Ingredients’ move from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA with RISE, this session will provide highlights on how CSM Ingredients saved on license costs, reduced risks, and improved compliance by focusing on SAP security.

 

As a follow-up from last year’s session, the presenter will explain how L’Oréal aligned its business processes with group directives through a business authorization model implemented in its Accounting and Finance shared service centers, and during the deployment of the group’s IGA tool.

L’Oréal

 

BP

BP is a regular presenter at our conference and always shares highly appreciated insights on the various aspects of their processes and tools. This year, BP will cover its journey of meeting its key Internal Control over Finance Reporting (ICFR) obligations and automating key control requirements using SAP GRC and how the team has transformed the control monitoring in real time across all business processes, running close to hundreds of business rules for as many control activities across the group.

 

This session will address Perfetti Van Melle’s journey from a reactive and manual GRC Maturity Model to an integrated, proactive and automated approach supported by SAP Access Control, SAP Process Control and SAP Audit Management solutions

Perfetti Van Melle

 

Wipro

Explaining how strategic focus on role design and access governance paved the way for improved risk management and sustained compliance, this session will provide insights into the steps, approaches, and lessons that contributed to building a resilient governance structure aligned with modern business needs.

 

SAP and partner led solution roadmaps, strategic updates and deep dives

 

Riscomp

Maxim Chupronov – Principal consultant and CEO of Riscomp and Vincent Doux – GRC & Security Customer Solution Advisor for EMEA at SAP, will provide an overview of SAP cybersecurity risk management and compliance, with a focus on key concepts like NIS2.

 

A regular feature of the conference, the “What’s New” sessions for SAP solutions for Three Lines and for Identity Access Management and Cybersecurity & Data Protection are back. With enhancements delivered regularly, it's easy to miss useful updates. SAP experts will provide the audience with a rapid overview of new capabilities recently delivered, as well as explanations and illustrations of select new features and functionality.

SAP

 

Where and when?

If you feel this agenda would be relevant to you, then join us on site!

  • Dates: March 11-12, 2025
  • Location: Budapest, Hungary
  • Venue: Marriott Budapest

 

How?

Well, that’s the easy part! Just head to the event website and registration page:

  • Event home page
  • Interactive agenda
  • Get your tickets!

 

Still not sure?

If you are still not sure if this is the right event, then allow me to share some of the great feedback that we have heard from last year:

I hope this gave you a taste for what is to come in Budapest, and I look forward to meeting you there. In the meantime, feel free to share your thoughts and comments either on this blog or on Twitter @TFrenehard

And if you are interested in learning more about SAP solutions for Governance, Risk, and Compliance, feel free to fill-in the demo request form!

entry.title: Re: Is "Amount Split" tab available in the Fiori screen o...
entry.content:
Hi Meri,
There is no "Amount Split" Tab in the app, could you help to clear where did you see the tab?

I can only see there is the manual amount split supported in the app Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced.
Please check the following link for further reference,
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/c7c55da4af0a49d08d7df04fc5b39138.html?locale=en-US&version=2502.500

Kind Regards,
Lucy

entry.title: Re: SAC - Data locks on this model prevented the changes...
entry.content: One possibility, user xxxx has unique data it's private/public edit which resulted in a slightly different target data region. Just a guess though...

entry.title: Differences between objectlist in CustomCodeLibrary and DecomissioningCockpit
entry.content:
Dear SAP support, 

 

we are using SAP CustiomCodeManagement on Solution Manager 7.2 SP13 FPS.

 

1: There are some objects in CCM, which are shown as CustomCode in the object list of the CCM Library, but not shown in the DecomissioningCockpit.

What could be the cause for this behavior?

 

2: There is a functionality called "Backup Transport" for exporting obsolete objects with multiselect option.

Is there also a functionality for deleting obsolete objects via the Solution Manager?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Differences between objectlist in CustomCodeLibrary a...
entry.content:
Dear *,

Regarding the question 1:
Can you confirm if a decommissioning project was created in the decommissioning cockpit according to the below KBA?
##3023133 - Best Practice: How to create a Decommissioning Analysis to identify unused objects

Regarding the question 2:
Within the Custom Code Management features of Solution Manager, you can identify, analyze, and act upon obsolete objects. After "Backup Transport", you can manually delete the obsolete objects in the managed system. There wasn’t a fully automated deletion feature for obsolete objects within Solution Manager. 

Best regards,
Leo
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Is "Amount Split" tab available in the Fiori screen o...
entry.content:
Hi Lucy

Yes, I saw it in the Create Supplier Invoice -  Advanced as well as in the Create Incoming invoice app.

entry.title: Re: Where can I find the results of the CALL CHECK_TABLE_...

Hi Shruti 

Check this SAP help portal link it will help you.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_ONE/1c837b3899834ddcbae140cc3e7c7bdd/2584ec2e324d44529edc8221956359ea.html

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: Is "Amount Split" tab available in the Fiori screen o...
entry.content: Thank you for this.

entry.title: Building Simple Networking App with Ease
entry.content:
Two weeks ago, SAP Go-To-Market-Kick-Off Meeting was held in Singapore. As part of APAC BTP team, I was tasked to build a mini networking application, so that it could be used in BTP dinner gathering, for BTP colleagues from different regions to network and get to know each other.

 

I was using only BTP components to build this networking application, with low-code SAP Build Apps to realise front-end design and functionalities, and pro-code SAP Build Code for back-end services, and SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database to store information. In collaboration with my colleague Png Xiao Qi, we managed to create a leaderboard by SAP Analytics Cloud to show live data streaming from the application, which modelled in SAP Datasphere.

The functionalities include:

  1. Create an user profile and retain user profile throughout
  2. Generate an unique QR code of the current user profile
  3. Scan other's QR to pop up profile infomation
  4. Show scanned profile and generate random ice-breaker question
  5. Save user profile and scanned profile to database
  6. Live leaderboard to show the most connected profile (Networ-KING)

 

So how did I use BTP to realise this UI and functionalities? Refer below for the UI, Back-end Services, Data Schema, and Dashboard:

 

 

 

 

 

Special thanks to my team (name in alphabetical order):

  • Front-end UI & Functionalities – Hannah Kong, Tan De Xun
  • Back-end Services – Tan De Xun
  • Data Model & Dashboard – Png Xiao Qi
  • SIT/UAT – Brenda Azali, Hannah Kong, Rachel Gan, Wang Jing Wen

If you have any query, please comment below!

entry.title: Re: How to open Custom fields tab in sales order (VA01/VA...
entry.content:
Hi,

i would go for a incompletion entry. Add the conditional entry via ABAP to the incompletion log.
Then on the custom UI for the additional fields control the input ready status of the field and navigation like this

  IF FEHLER = 'X'.
    SET CURSOR FIELD FEHLERFELD.
  ENDIF.

 

Kind regards

Robert

entry.title: Re: Flexi Workflow WS00800157- Multi level approval
entry.content: Hello, How to configure a flexible workflow for above scenario? I have the same requirement.

entry.title: Default Values in the Search Input of Cost Center Hierarchy in MDG
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have requirement to default the value of the Edition, controlling area and Cost center hierarchy in the attached screenshot. I tried to check the feed class but the feeder class is not available in the technical help. Any suggestion or hits are highly appreciated.

Thanks!!

entry.title: Re: Parked Invoice for specific suppliers
entry.content:
Yes, you can set up a system to automatically park invoices from specific suppliers that you consider problematic. Here's how you can approach this:

  1. Supplier Identification: First, you need to identify and mark the suppliers you consider problematic in your SAP system.

  2. Invoice Validation: When an invoice is received from these marked suppliers, the system can be configured to automatically park the invoice instead of posting it directly [1].

  3. Workflow Setup: You can set up a workflow for these parked invoices, which will require manual intervention and additional validation before they can be posted [1].

  4. Additional Information: Users will need to add the required information to validate the invoice before it can be processed further [1].

  5. Automated Parking: While the standard process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud typically posts invoices directly, especially those from the SAP Ariba Supplier Network, you may need to explore extensibility options to implement automatic parking for specific suppliers [2].

  6. Manual Review: Once parked, these invoices can be reviewed in the "Supplier Invoice List" application, where you can see their status and manage them accordingly [3].

By implementing this approach, you can ensure that invoices from suppliers you've identified as problematic are automatically parked for further review and validation, rather than being posted directly into the system.

entry.title: Re: MEASUREMENT POINT MAINTENANCE PLAN DUE BASED ON LAST...
entry.content:
Greetings,

you need to maintain shift factors on maintenance scheduling parameters tab. Maintain both at 100%.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: MTA App with UI5 - Running same application isolated...
entry.content:
Interesting use case! Just wondering why you’re not using multiple subaccounts? Ideally you have a subaccount for each backend system to have tenant isolation.

Nevertheless, this should be possible. What do you use as value for sap.cloud.service in the manifest file? This needs to be different.

how does the configuration looks like for the html5 app repository?

once it works you can also use the transport management service to transport between spaces in the same subaccount with a different mta extension file for each space.

entry.title: Re: Problem with the CDS View "searchable" calculated column / case.. when column
entry.content: Hi,did you get the answer for this ?If yes can you please post it here.

entry.title: Re: Adaptation of SAP Standard Fiori App: Creating a custom button and displaying table data by usin
entry.content:
Hi @23_Suman 

Here ListReportExtension is actually the extension controller name only.

In your example, try to replace <<app2.new.newButtonCont>> to <<newButtonCont>>.
It should work.

entry.title: Re: New Videos on DMS with SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Can you show some screenshots and explain the flow of that objectId? For example, I assume you have a process that calls an automation, and the automation returns an objectId. Where is the variable you mean?  How do you set it?

It just sounds like the value is not being passed back to the form.

entry.title: Re: Where can I find the results of the CALL CHECK_TABLE_...
entry.content:
Hello shruti,

Normally CHECK_TABLE_CONSISTENCY should give you result in table once you execute with list of tables and errors, if table is empty all tables are good.

You can also check : GLOBAL_TABLE_CONSISTENCY in _SYS_STATISTICS .

Regards

Sagar

entry.title: Adding cost center Hierarchy field in the cost center search screen in SAP MDG
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have requirement to add the Hierarchy assignment field in the cost center display screen. When we search for any cost center we need to add the Hierarchy assignment field to be displayed along with the other cost center fields.  Any suggestion or hits, how can we achieve this are highly appreciated.

 

Thanks!!

entry.title: Re: First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher)
entry.content:
Hi Maurizio, 

You only need to modify the import buffer manually when really there is a need for this. What I was trying to explain is that from the Feature we trigger the tp import commands to the TMS, that CALM has not any option to modify the import order as TMS is the "master" of the transport requests, the master of the transport routes.

Best regards,

Dolores

A Rate Plan Template is created, when creating rate plan, I have to select the rate plan template and create one. Whenever I create a product, I am given an option to create rate plan instead of selecting the already created one in "Manage Rate Plan" app.

Could you please confirm if there's a button to select already available rate plans from "Manage Rate Plan" app?

 

Thanks

Prasannakrishna Singeetham

entry.title: Can someone suggest on the significance of Indicator field in GRACFFUSER table in SAP GRC
entry.content: For some users GRACFFUSER shows X in Indicator field in SAP GRC. Kindly suggest what does it mean. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Change of bank key in banks and house bank
entry.content:
To change the bank key from a random legacy system value to the SWIFT/BIC code and update the bank account structure in your S/4 HANA public cloud implementation project, follow these steps:

  1. Create a new bank master record:

    • Use the "Manage Banks" app to create a new bank with the SWIFT/BIC code as the bank key [1].
    • Set up the bank name, address, SWIFT code, bank branch, and bank number [1].

  2. Set up a new house bank:

    • In the "Manage Banks" app, search for the newly created bank record [1].
    • Click on the bank name to display the record [1].
    • Go to the House Bank tab and click "Add Record" [1].
    • Enter the new House Bank ID and Company Code, then save [1].

  3. Create a new bank account number:

    • Use the "Manage Bank Accounts" app and click "Add Record" [1].
    • Enter the new account details, including Account Number, Description, Bank Country, and Currency [1].
    • Select the newly created bank key from the drop-down menu [1].
    • In the General Data section, enter Company Code, Account Holder, Account Type, and Opening Date [1].
    • If applicable, maintain IBAN and Bank Control Key [1].

  4. Link the new house bank account:

    • In the "House Bank Connectivity" tab, click "Add Record" [1].
    • Enter the Company Code, select the new House Bank from the drop-down, and maintain the House Bank Account ID [1].
    • In the House Bank Account Data section, select the Main Bank G/L Account number [1].

  5. Update bank determination configuration:

    • After creating the new house bank account, update the settings in the configuration activity "Set Up Bank Determination for Payment Transactions" [2].

  6. Handle existing transactions:

    • For the two bank accounts with existing transactions in DEV and TEST, you may need to create a migration strategy to move these transactions to the new account structure.

  7. Update payment methods:

    • Revise the payment method configuration to use the new bank account details [3].

  8. Block or delete old accounts:

    • Once all transactions are migrated, block or delete the old bank accounts to prevent further use [4].

  9. Remove old house bank and account ID:

    • After ensuring all processes are using the new structure, remove the old house bank and account ID configurations [4].

  10. Update electronic bank configuration:

    • If necessary, adjust the electronic bank configuration to reflect the new account structure [4].

Remember to test these changes thoroughly in your DEV and TEST environments before implementing them in production. If you encounter errors related to items already in use, you may need to carefully migrate existing data or transactions to the new structure before removing the old one.

entry.title: Re: Integration from SAP Sales Cloud V2 to S4HC Public Ed...
entry.content:
Hi @KhaingZinTun 

I searched in the help portal and api hub, and found the following information.

[DEPRECATED] SAP Service Cloud Version 2 for Utilities Integration with SAP S/4HANA
***This integration package is deprecated. The alternative for this integration package are listed below:
SAP Service Cloud V2 for Utilities Premise Integration with SAP S/4HANA
SAP Service Cloud V2 for Utilities Contract Account Integration with SAP S/4HANA
SAP Service Cloud V2 for Utilities Master Data Integration with SAP S/4HANA***

SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 Integration Guide for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Could you please check if the alternative integration package listed can fufill your requirement?

Best regards,
Irene

entry.title: Re: Close Group Tree button is hidden in Crystal report v...
entry.content:
Thank You for the confirmation!!

We have noticed couple of additional issues in SP 37 regarding group tree.
1. Vertical scroll bar is not visible in SP 37, whereas in SP 35 it is visible.
2. Horizontal scroll bar is visible but it is not fixed at one position. In SP 35, it is fixed at bottom of the group tree.

Please let me know if these issues will also be resolved in SP 39. Thanks again!!

entry.title: Update procedure for SAP Public Cloud version and tool to validate the business influence
entry.content:
Recently, the SAP public cloud has released new version 2502. According to the updates, I have some doubts:

1. What is the standard procedure to update the system?

Usually if we need to change the system, we need to change the configuration from customizing system, to test system and then to production system.

Do the SAP version updates also follow the same sequence and generate Customizing Transports? 

 

2. Is there any tool to validate or test the business influence caused by the updates?

If the SAP version updates bring some changes which may have impact on our current business, how can we test or validate these influence? Is there any recommended tool? 

I am looking forward to reply. Thank you for all answers in advance.

entry.title: Re: How to track the person responsible or custodian of t...
entry.content:
To track who is responsible for an asset, such as a laptop distributed to an employee, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can consider the following approaches:

  1. Asset Master Data:

    • Use custom fields in the asset master data to record the employee responsible for the asset [1].
    • This could be implemented by adding a field for the employee's Personnel Number (PERNR) to the asset record [1].

  2. Asset Tracking:

    • Implement a custom solution or enhance existing functionality to track asset movements and assignments [2].
    • This could include processes for asset delivery, return, and transfer between branches or employees [2].

  3. Integration with Human Resources:

    • Link the asset management system with HR data to associate assets with specific employees [3].
    • This integration can help in tracking which employee is responsible for which asset based on their personnel number or user ID [3].

  4. Authorization and Security:

    • Implement security measures to ensure that only authorized personnel can approve asset assignments or removals from company premises [4].
    • Set up approval workflows for asset borrowing requests [4].

  5. Asset Manager Monitoring:

    • Provide functionality for asset managers to monitor the status and location of assets within the company [4].
    • This could include reports or dashboards showing which assets are assigned to which employees.

  6. Custom Development:

    • Since standard Asset Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition may not provide all the required authorization checks and tracking features, custom development might be necessary to fully meet the requirement [4].

It's important to note that while SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides robust asset management capabilities, the specific functionality for tracking individual asset responsibility to employees may require a combination of standard features and custom enhancements to fully meet your

entry.title: Re: Change of bank key in banks and house bank
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question. Here's what the expert replied:

Ideally, bank key (BNKA-BANKL) can’t be changed since this is primary key of bank master. User can change bank number (BNKA-BNKLZ) and BIC (BNKA-SWIFT) code or create a new bank master with different bank key.

If new bank master is created, then the idea is to create new bank account, house bank, house bank account. For the existing bank accounts, customer can close them if they are no longer valid.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest Winners Revealed
entry.content: Congratulations to all Devtoberfest winners! 🎉

entry.title: REFX integration PM/ FSM
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Hope you are doing well...!

I have an inquiry regarding the integration of PM and REFX. We seek to establish a method for sharing the functional location to effectively track maintenance services related to specific objects (Rental Object), such as vehicle repairs and hotel equipment repairs (including bed and air conditioning replacements for particular requests). Our intention is to record maintenance charges through the FSM (Field service management) module rather than the REFX module.

If we aim to activate the PM module for the functional location assigned to each objective within the FSM (Field Service Management), what prerequisites should we consider? Please note that service charge settlements are not utilized for capturing maintenance costs, as these charges are independent of the contracts. I kindly request your expertise on this matter.

Thank you for great support...!

Best Regards,

Sayeesh

Regards

Gavan

SAP Technical Support, Public Cloud ERP

entry.title: IP address/s of no-reply-cpq@notifications.sap.com
entry.content:
Based on sap note 3292333, our network team can setup white listing for no-reply-cpq@notifications.sap.com to ensure the CPQ emails will be received correctly.

Could you please provide the IP address of this email in our PRD, QA, and DEV tenants?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: IP address/s of no-reply-cpq@notifications.sap.com
entry.content:
There is no particular IP address for "no-reply-cpq@notifications.sap.com".

As per the help document Outbound SAP CPQ Emails | SAP Help Portal, you would need to whitelist all of the IP addresses that belong to your environment's data center.

entry.title: SAP DMS and Destination Help for CAP service.
entry.content:
Scenario: We have 2 sub-account, sub-account A and sub-account B in SAP BTP Cockpit under a global account, all the development is being done on SAP BAS.

Sub-account A: This account has a DMS service which is currently being consumed by an app.
Sub-account B: This account is our development account in which we do our development.

Problem: The DMS service needs to be called to Sub-account B from Sub-account A for making an attachment functionality in our application. To consume the DMS, I need to create a CAP service which has the functionality of List all the content of the repository of the DMS, Upload content into the repo and download content from the repo.

Currently, I have attempted Destination Config and hardcoding the credentials for Bearer token authorization but I am getting 401 unauthorized error when trying to call the DMS service using destination and Method not allowed error when trying to use hard-code the credentials (which i know is not a recommended practice but I had to test it) but both these efforts have been in vain. 

Can somebody share their expertise on this topic, If its even possible to do something like this: Creating a CAP service which can download,upload and List the content of a DMS repository which is situated in a different Sub-account under the same global account. 
Kindly Help if you can. Thank you

entry.title: Re: BW4HANA Open ODS view - master data
entry.content: Thank you. Can you please explain the "requiring explicit filtering in queries"? what must be done so the validity fields in validity folders are taken into consideration.

We have actually tested with the other unit of measurement (LB), which is also a pre-delivered unit and we were able to edit the decimal places. Is this process not recommended?

Best regards,

Fujita

Now passing that Object ID to this automation

Expanding variable to show what I mentioned





Showing upload file datatype to pass in approval form

entry.title: cannot change ABAP program

entry.title: Re: [R] APP职能范围-实际丢失（R）
entry.content:
the Functional Areas - Actuals app is deprecated as of SAP S/4HANA 2408 and will be deleted from the SAP Fiori launchpad in an upcoming release.

The app is replaced by a successor app called P&L - Actuals (App ID: F0958).

Hope you enjoy!

Julie Plummer.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Viewer inside UpdatePanel displays onl...
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes, I did manually change the Crystal Report Viewer properties as per our requirement.

I am facing this issue only when I have BestFitPage property as false and Crystal Report Viewer is inside an Update Panel. If I set BestFitPage to true, report is displayed successfully. However, report alignment gets changed and it does not match with our UI requirement. So we had to set - BestFitPage="False" Width="100%" Height="100%".

entry.title: Re: Fiori Reuse Library Application Log 'script load error'
entry.content: I have sap hana system and already deployed my app to the system. But still the deployed app trying to find reused component (changedocscomponent) to resources? I already put in the manifest.json in the dependency libs
